title: Apple sues OpenAI for alleged theft of trade secrets with help of recruited employees
summary: Apple accuses OpenAI of orchestrating a campaign for former employees to take confidential hardware information.

title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.6 with Sol, Terra and Luna models and demonstrates advanced reasoning capabilities
summary: The new version includes five reasoning levels, autonomous post-training and a mathematical demonstration of a conjecture.

title: OpenAI closes its Atlas browser after eight months and unifies all its tools in ChatGPT
summary: The company simplifies its product strategy by integrating the browser and launching a new work tool in ChatGPT.

title: SK Hynix raises $26.5 billion in largest foreign IPO in the US
summary: The South Korean memory company plans to expand HBM manufacturing and build new factories in the US with the funds.

title: Micron increases its investment in the US to $250 billion to meet AI demand
summary: The company takes a stake in the country's only 300 mm wafer plant to boost memory chip production.

title: Intel Core Ultra 5 245K hits all-time low price dropping to $179 on Amazon
summary: The mid-range processor with 14 cores and PCIe 5.0 support is offered with a discount of up to 42% off its usual price.

title: NVIDIA prepares GeForce RTX 5090 SE as an intermediate version between the RTX 5080 and RTX 5090
summary: The graphics card would be placed in a segment between the two high-end ranges, according to industry reports.

title: They manage to run Minecraft in 3D with textures on an original Game Boy and Game Boy Color
summary: A developer manages to render the game on hardware from almost 30 years ago through extreme compression and optimization techniques.

title: Florida ransom negotiator sentenced to 70 months in prison for aiding BlackCat ransomware
summary: The defendant facilitated negotiations between victims and the ransomware group, being sentenced for extortion.

title: Tencent negotiates to buy majority of Manus from Meta after Chinese government intervention
summary: The Manus platform hopes to maintain its independence while the Chinese tech giant seeks to acquire the social network.

title: Grok 4.5 and Muse Spark 1.1 models offer higher performance at a significantly lower price
summary: Muse Spark 1.1 extends its context to 1 million tokens and improves orchestration of agentic tasks and code generation.

title: EU threatens Meta with fines of up to 12 billion for addictive design of Instagram and Facebook
summary: Brussels considers the platforms addictive by design and demands changes in compliance with digital regulations.

title: OpenAI's head of security leaves the company
summary: The security director leaves the company at a key moment for the development of artificial intelligence.

title: Hugging Face CEO explains why companies no longer rent their AI
summary: Companies are opting for their own AI solutions instead of relying on external services, according to the executive.

title: Oatomic raises $300 million to build a viable quantum computer with 20,000 qubits
summary: The startup plans to develop a quantum computer that can solve practical problems with a limited number of qubits.

title: China catches up to Elon Musk's reusable rockets, according to a report
summary: The country advances in the development of reusable rocket technology, directly competing with SpaceX.

title: AMD confirms Zen 6 launches in less than two weeks, starting with EPYC Venice
summary: The new server CPU architecture will first arrive on the EPYC platform, with consumer versions later.

title: Asha Sharma lays off 3,200 Xbox employees and is appointed employment and productivity advisor in the US
summary: The executive cut a large number of jobs in Microsoft's video game division and was later appointed as a government advisor.

title: European Parliament approves Chat Control 1.0 allowing scanning of user data without a court order
summary: The controversial regulation sneaks into the European Parliament allowing companies to scan private communications without a court order.

title: Anthropic claims it can read Claude's 'thoughts' according to a new research paper
summary: The study reveals that language models have a global workspace that allows interpreting their reasoning process.
